TS-001: git commit -m '$(cat <<EOF...)' FAILS in PowerShell 5.1 — heredoc is bash-only. WORKS: Write message to temp file then `git commit -F tempfile`. OR use PowerShell here-string written to file first. Shell: PowerShell 5.1, Platform: Windows.

TS-002: `A && B` FAILS in PowerShell 5.1 — && is not a valid statement separator. WORKS: Use semicolons: `A; if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { B }`. OR upgrade to pwsh 7+ where && works natively. Critical for any chained commands in Knight sessions on Windows.

TS-011: PREVENTIVE PROTOCOL — Step 0: when creating ANY new directory or file under librarian-mcp/ or BISHOP_DROPZONE/, FIRST command must be `git check-ignore -v <new-path>`. If it returns a rule, address BEFORE staging. Root .gitignore has blanket rules (*.py, *.json, *.yaml, *.md, *.txt, *.ps1) that catch most files; specific carve-outs needed. Add negation to root .gitignore, commit the .gitignore change BEFORE adding files.

TS-015: Python Unicode print() fails with UnicodeEncodeError in Windows PowerShell (cp1252 encoding). Affects non-ASCII chars: arrows (->), checkmarks, box-drawing chars. FIX: (A) Replace all non-ASCII with ASCII equivalents in print statements. OR (B) Set PYTHONUTF8=1 before running: `$env:PYTHONUTF8=1; python script.py`. Option B preferred for many Unicode symbols.

TS-036: New .py/.ps1/.jsonl files under librarian-mcp/scripts/ or librarian-mcp/stitchpunks/data/ are silently ignored by root *.py rule (~line 232 of .gitignore). Per-file negation (!librarian-mcp/scripts/<filename>.py) needed. Verify with `git check-ignore -v <path>`. K507 precedent: added carve-outs for pawn_with_substrate.py, pawn_with_substrate.ps1, substrate_savings_log.jsonl, synapse_K507.jsonl. Exception block lives around line 404+ in root .gitignore.

TS-039: MCP run_session_start times out when total wall time exceeds default ~30s.
